title: Project skeleton, pinned deps & lint gate
description:
## Goal
A green-building crate skeleton with the strict AGENTS.md lint gate and pinned dependencies.

## Scope
- In: Cargo.toml deps pinned (agent-client-protocol 0.13.x +unstable, -tokio, tokio, reqwest+reqwest-eventsource, serde/serde_json, ignore/grep/globset, uuid, tracing(+subscriber), clap, thiserror; dev: test-log, proptest); crate-root lints (#![forbid(unsafe_code)], deny pedantic, no unwrap/expect/panic/todo); clap `serve` subcommand stub; tracing init.
- Out: any ACP/DeepSeek behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo build/test/fmt --check/clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings all pass on the skeleton.
2. `cargo doc --no-deps` has no undocumented public API.
3. `serve` subcommand exists and exits cleanly.

## Testing
- A trivial passing test; CI-equivalent gate runs locally.

## Notes
- Blocks all other tasks. Run `cargo audit` before adding deps.
close_reason: Implemented green skeleton with pinned deps, strict lint gate, serve subcommand, docs, and a passing test. Kept direct tokio runtime dependency but did not add agent-client-protocol-tokio because the only published helper is 0.11.1 and pulls duplicate ACP 0.11 types.

title: Local tools: list_dir, glob, grep
description:
## Goal
Local code-navigation tools the ACP client fs surface does not provide.

## Scope
- In: list_dir(path), glob(pattern) via globset, grep(regex) via ignore+grep (ripgrep engine), respecting .gitignore; bounded/truncated results with a note when capped.
- Out: client-side execution (these are always local).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. grep returns matches with file:line; glob matches expected paths; list_dir lists entries.
2. Results are bounded and signal truncation.

## Testing
- Temp-dir fixtures; assert matches, ignore handling, truncation note.

## Notes
- Depends on the tool loop. Parallel with read_file.

title: DeepSeek-backed coding agent over ACP
description:
## Problem Statement

ACP-speaking editors (Zed, neovim/CodeCompanion) can drive Claude and Codex as coding agents, but not DeepSeek. DeepSeek exposes only a chat-completions API (tool-calling + streaming) with no agentic engine, so no existing adapter turns it into a full ACP coding agent. The one DeepSeek+ACP precedent (CodeWhale) is TUI-first and exposes only text prompt/response over ACP — tool-backed editing and permissions are not surfaced.

## Solution

A headless, ACP-first adapter binary that presents DeepSeek V4 as a coding agent to any ACP client. We build the agent harness ourselves (system prompt, tool catalog, the prompt→tool-call→execute→feed-back loop, conversation history, permission gating) on top of DeepSeek's streaming API, and crucially surface tool calls, edits and permission prompts THROUGH ACP — the part prior art skipped. The editor is the only UI.

## Architecture

Two independent channels. Editor⇄adapter: JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, handled by the agent-client-protocol crate (the official rust-sdk). Adapter⇄DeepSeek: HTTPS + SSE to the OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint, via a thin client we own. The adapter is the translator + agent loop in the middle. A translation boundary keeps ACP/HTTP types out of the harness core (AGENTS.md: business logic must not depend on I/O).

## Scope (features / milestones)

Tracer-bullet sequence, each demoable in a real editor: M0 connects (streaming text), M1 reads (read-only tool loop), M2 edits + permissions (= v0.1 release line), M3 runs (hybrid shell), M4 polish (model picker, images, docs).

## Out of Scope

No TUI. No MCP client support, no loadSession, no auto model-router, no apply_patch-style edits in v0.1.

## Key Constraints

Strict AGENTS.md: forbid unsafe; clippy pedantic; no unwrap/expect/panic/todo; minimal audited deps; documented public APIs; never log secrets.

title: Dev CLI: drive the agent over stdio for e2e testing
description:
## Goal
A developer harness to exercise the agent without an editor.

## Scope
- In: a hidden `dev` subcommand (or test bin) that pipes JSON-RPC frames to the running agent over stdio and prints responses/updates; a scripted initialize→new→prompt smoke flow.
- Out: a real TUI.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Running the dev CLI against the agent completes an initialize→session/new→session/prompt round-trip and prints streamed updates.

## Testing
- Smoke: dev CLI drives a prompt end-to-end (mock or real backend behind a flag).

## Notes
- Depends on the prompt turn. Priority 2 (tooling aid).

title: M3: Shell command execution (hybrid terminal)
description:
## Problem Statement

Many coding tasks need to run commands (build, test, git). Zed offers terminal callbacks; CodeCompanion does not advertise terminal capability — so a single mechanism will not work across both targets.

## Solution

Add run_command: use client terminal/* when advertised (Zed), fall back to local tokio::process when not (CodeCompanion), permission-gated like other mutations.

## User Stories

1. As a Zed user, I want the agent to run commands in the editor terminal, so that I see output natively.
2. As a CodeCompanion user, I want commands to still run (locally), so that the agent works despite no terminal capability.
3. As a user, I want to approve commands, so that nothing destructive runs without consent.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given clientCapabilities.terminal, when run_command executes, then it uses terminal/create + output/wait_for_exit/release and surfaces output.
2. Given no terminal capability, then it runs via local tokio::process and reports output through tool_call_update.
3. Given run_command in ask/accept-edits mode, then it requires permission; cancellation kills the child process.

## Implementation Notes

- Capability detection chooses terminal vs local at runtime. Cancellation aborts running child processes.

## Testing Notes

- Both paths (terminal advertised vs not); cancellation kills the child.

## Out of Scope

Background/long-running terminal session management beyond a single command; model picker; images.

title: session/cancel: abort SSE & flush, return cancelled
description:
## Goal
Real cancellation that aborts the in-flight DeepSeek stream.

## Scope
- In: cancellation token plumbed through the turn; session/cancel aborts the live SSE request, flushes pending session/update, returns StopReason::cancelled (not an error).
- Out: cancelling child processes (M3).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Given an in-flight turn, session/cancel aborts the SSE stream promptly.
2. Pending updates are flushed before session/prompt returns StopReason::cancelled.

## Testing
- Mock LlmClient that streams slowly; cancel mid-stream; assert cancelled + flushed updates + no error surfaced.

## Notes
- Depends on the prompt turn.

title: Tool registry + agentic tool-call loop
description:
## Goal
Generalize the turn into a tool-call loop with reporting and a safety cap.

## Scope
- In: tool JSON-schema registry passed in the DeepSeek tools param; accumulate streamed tool_call args by index; dispatch; report tool_call(pending)+tool_call_update(completed/failed); feed results back; iterate until no tool calls or iteration cap (default ~25) → StopReason::max_turn_requests.
- Out: concrete tools (separate tasks).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Streamed tool_calls are reassembled correctly and dispatched.
2. Each call emits tool_call then tool_call_update; results are appended to history and sent back to the model.
3. The iteration cap trips returning max_turn_requests.

## Testing
- Mock model emitting fragmented tool_calls across deltas; assert reassembly, update sequence, cap behavior.

## Notes
- Core M1 infra. Depends on the M0 prompt turn.

title: DeepSeek LlmClient trait + reqwest/SSE impl
description:
## Goal
A thin streaming DeepSeek client behind an LlmClient trait (the mock seam).

## Scope
- In: LlmClient trait; reqwest + reqwest-eventsource impl of /chat/completions stream:true; env config (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/BASE_URL/MODEL, default deepseek-v4-pro); parse text + reasoning_content deltas; clear error if key missing; never log the key.
- Out: tool-call parsing (M1), images (M4).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Given canned SSE, the client yields ordered text + reasoning deltas and a finish reason.
2. Missing API key returns a clear typed error; the key never appears in tracing output.

## Testing
- Mock transport / fixture SSE; assert delta ordering and finish mapping; assert no key in logs.

## Notes
- Depends on skeleton. Can run in parallel with initialize.
close_reason: Implemented DeepSeek client trait and streaming SSE parsing

title: README & setup docs (env config + GUI env gotcha + client snippets)
description:
## Goal
First-run docs so the adapter is usable in Zed and CodeCompanion.

## Scope
- In: README covering env-only config (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY/BASE_URL/MODEL), the GUI env-inheritance gotcha (use agent_servers.env in Zed), Zed and CodeCompanion config snippets, supported tools/modes, and current limitations (no MCP/loadSession).
- Out: published website.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. README documents config, the GUI env gotcha workaround, and working Zed + CodeCompanion setup snippets.

## Testing
- Manual: following the README configures the agent in both editors.

## Notes
- Capstone; depends on model selection + image input so the full feature set is documented.

title: read_file tool (client fs + local fallback)
description:
## Goal
Let the model read files, preferring the editor's buffer-aware fs.

## Scope
- In: read_file(path, optional line range); route to client fs/read_text_file when fs.readTextFile is advertised, else local read; bounded output.
- Out: writing/editing.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. With fs.readTextFile advertised, reads go through the client (respecting unsaved buffers).
2. Without it, a local read is used. Line ranges honored.

## Testing
- Fake client advertising/not advertising fs; assert routing and content.

## Notes
- Depends on the tool loop.

title: M0: Protocol bootstrap & streaming text chat
description:
## Problem Statement

Nothing yet lets an ACP editor talk to DeepSeek. We need the end-to-end pipe working: editor handshake → prompt → live streamed DeepSeek answer.

## Solution

Implement the ACP Agent trait for the initialize/session lifecycle and a text-only streaming turn: session/prompt calls DeepSeek with stream:true and forwards token deltas as session/update chunks, maps reasoning_content to thought chunks, and honors cancellation.

## User Stories

1. As a Zed/CodeCompanion user, I want to open a session and send a prompt, so that DeepSeek's answer streams live into my editor.
2. As a user, I want reasoning shown separately from the answer, so that I can follow the model's thinking.
3. As a user, I want to cancel a running turn, so that I can stop a long or wrong answer immediately.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a client connects, when it calls initialize, then the agent negotiates protocol version and advertises capabilities (no auth method; prompt caps text+embeddedContext+image; loadSession false) and stores the client's capabilities.
2. Given a new session, when session/prompt is sent, then DeepSeek is called with stream:true and text deltas arrive as agent_message_chunk updates.
3. Given the model returns reasoning_content, then it is forwarded as agent_thought_chunk updates.
4. Given a turn in flight, when session/cancel arrives, then the live SSE request is aborted and the turn returns StopReason::cancelled after flushing pending updates.
5. Given the model finishes, then session/prompt returns StopReason::end_turn (or max_tokens).

## Implementation Notes

- Depend on agent-client-protocol (0.13.x pinned, unstable feature) + -tokio; tokio multi-thread runtime.
- Thin DeepSeek client (reqwest + reqwest-eventsource); model/base_url/key from env; never log the key.
- Translation boundary; LlmClient trait is the mock seam.
- Cancellation via a tokio cancellation token that aborts the SSE stream.

## Testing Notes

- Mock LlmClient feeding canned SSE deltas; in-memory fake ACP Client capturing updates. Cover all returned StopReasons including cancelled.

## Out of Scope

Tools, permissions, editing, shell, model picker; images are declared in caps but not exercised here.

title: M2: File editing with permissions over ACP (v0.1)
description:
## Problem Statement

The whole point of the adapter: let DeepSeek edit code with the user in control. Prior DeepSeek+ACP art does not surface edits or permissions through ACP. This feature is the v0.1 release line.

## Solution

Add write_file and edit_file (string-replace) via client fs, gated by session/request_permission, with permission-posture modes (ask / accept-edits / yolo) switchable via session/set_mode. Edits render as native diffs in the editor.

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want the agent to edit files, so that it can implement changes.
2. As a user, I want to approve or reject each edit, so that I stay in control.
3. As a user, I want to choose a posture (ask / accept-edits / yolo), so that I trade safety for speed.
4. As a user, I want edits shown as diffs, so that I can review them in my editor.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a mutating tool in ask mode, when the agent wants to run it, then it first calls session/request_permission offering allow_once/allow_always/reject_once/reject_always and proceeds only on allow.
2. Given allow_always for a tool, then later calls of that tool in the same session are not re-prompted.
3. Given edit_file, then the file is read (via client fs) first, the exact old_string is replaced, and the result written via fs/write_text_file (native diff shown); a missing or non-unique old_string fails the tool with a clear error fed back to the model.
4. Given mode=accept-edits edits auto-allow but shell still prompts; mode=yolo auto-allows all; session/set_mode switches posture live.

## Implementation Notes

- String-replace edit (NOT apply_patch) — DeepSeek is weaker at exact patch syntax; the write still goes through client fs so the editor renders the diff.
- Permission memory keyed per-tool per-session.
- Modes advertised in session/new (currentModeId/availableModes).

## Testing Notes

- proptest the string-replace (apply-then-read-back invariants); no-match/ambiguous-match cases. Permission gating + allow_always memory; mode posture matrix.

## Out of Scope

Shell/terminal, model picker, image input, MCP.

title: run_command via client terminal/* when advertised (Zed)
description:
## Goal
Host commands in the editor's terminal when the client supports it.

## Scope
- In: when clientCapabilities.terminal is advertised, run_command uses terminal/create + terminal/output + wait_for_exit + release; capability detection selects this over the local path.
- Out: persistent/background terminals.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. With terminal advertised, run_command uses the terminal/* methods and surfaces output/exit.
2. Without it, falls back to the local path (unchanged).

## Testing
- Fake client advertising terminal; assert terminal/* call sequence; assert fallback when absent.

## Notes
- Depends on the local shell task (shares the run_command tool).

title: run_command via local process (permission-gated, cancellable)
description:
## Goal
Run shell commands locally so the agent works on clients without terminal capability (CodeCompanion).

## Scope
- In: run_command(cmd) via tokio::process; capture stdout/stderr/exit; permission-gated; reported via tool_call_update; cancellation kills the child.
- Out: editor-hosted terminal (next task).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. With no terminal capability, run_command executes locally and reports output + exit code.
2. session/cancel kills the running child process.

## Testing
- Run a deterministic command; assert output/exit; cancel a long command and assert the child is killed.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate. Extends cancellation (M0) to child processes.

title: Model selection: unstable set_model + availableModels (+ env default)
description:
## Goal
Let capable clients pick the model in-session; everyone else uses the env default.

## Scope
- In: advertise availableModels (v4-flash, v4-pro, v4-pro+thinking) and handle session/set_model behind the crate's unstable feature; DEEPSEEK_MODEL is the initial/default; graceful no-op for clients lacking the API.
- Out: auto model-router.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A client supporting set_model can switch the active model mid-session; the next DeepSeek call uses it.
2. A client without it runs on DEEPSEEK_MODEL (default deepseek-v4-pro) with no error.

## Testing
- Fake clients with/without set_model; assert switching vs default.

## Notes
- Depends on the M0 prompt turn (independent of tools).

title: edit_file string-replace tool (+ proptest)
description:
## Goal
Reliable targeted edits via exact string replacement.

## Scope
- In: edit_file(path, old_string, new_string): read via client fs, replace the unique exact occurrence, write via client fs; error clearly on no-match or non-unique match; result fed back to the model.
- Out: apply_patch/unified-diff format (explicitly out per plan).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A unique old_string is replaced and written via client fs (native diff).
2. Absent or ambiguous (non-unique) old_string fails with a descriptive error; the file is unchanged.

## Testing
- proptest: apply-then-read-back invariants; unit tests for no-match and ambiguous-match.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate and read_file. String-replace, not patch (DeepSeek is weaker at patch syntax).

title: session/new + streaming text prompt turn
description:
## Goal
A text-only prompt turn: session/prompt streams DeepSeek output to the editor.

## Scope
- In: session/new (in-memory session: history, cwd, mode); session/prompt → call DeepSeek (stream) → forward text as agent_message_chunk, reasoning as agent_thought_chunk; append to history; return StopReason end_turn/max_tokens.
- Out: tools, permissions, cancellation (next task).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. session/new returns a session id and advertised modes.
2. A prompt streams agent_message_chunk updates; reasoning maps to thought chunks; turn returns end_turn (or max_tokens).
3. Conversation history is retained per session and replayed on the next prompt.

## Testing
- Mock LlmClient + fake ACP client; assert update sequence, StopReason, and history replay.

## Notes
- Depends on initialize + DeepSeek client.

title: Permission gate: session/request_permission + allow_always memory
description:
## Goal
Gate mutating tools behind explicit user approval.

## Scope
- In: before any mutating tool, call session/request_permission offering allow_once/allow_always/reject_once/reject_always; proceed only on allow; remember allow_always per-tool per-session; read-only tools never prompt.
- Out: mode posture (next task), the mutating tools themselves.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A mutating tool in default posture prompts before executing and respects the chosen option.
2. allow_always suppresses re-prompts for that tool for the session; reject aborts the tool with a result fed back to the model.

## Testing
- Fake client returning each option kind; assert gating + allow_always memory + reject handling.

## Notes
- Depends on the tool loop. Core M2 infra.

title: M1: Read-only tool loop
description:
## Problem Statement

A chat-only agent cannot investigate a codebase. The model must read files and search before it can help.

## Solution

Add the agentic tool loop with read-only tools: read_file (via client fs when advertised, else local), list_dir/glob/grep (local), each reported through tool_call/tool_call_update updates. No permission prompts (read-only).

## User Stories

1. As a user, I want the agent to read files, so that its answers reflect my actual code.
2. As a user, I want the agent to search/glob/list the project, so that it locates relevant code on its own.
3. As a user, I want each tool call shown in my editor, so that I can see what the agent is doing.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given DeepSeek returns tool_calls for read-only tools, when the loop runs, then each is reported as tool_call (pending) then tool_call_update (completed) and the result is fed back to the model.
2. Given read_file and client advertises fs.readTextFile, then the read goes through fs/read_text_file (respecting unsaved buffers); otherwise a local read.
3. Given grep/glob/list_dir, then they execute locally (ripgrep engine / globset) and return bounded results.
4. Given the model issues further tool calls, then the loop iterates until none remain, or the iteration cap (default ~25) trips returning StopReason::max_turn_requests.

## Implementation Notes

- Tool JSON-schema registry passed in the DeepSeek tools param; accumulate streamed tool-call arguments by index.
- Search via ignore + grep crates; glob via globset.
- Capability-driven routing of read_file (client vs local).

## Testing Notes

- Mock model emitting tool calls; assert the tool_call/tool_call_update sequence and history feedback. Test the max_turn_requests cap.

## Out of Scope

Mutating tools, permissions, shell.

title: M4: Model selection, image input & docs polish
description:
## Problem Statement

v0.1 runs a fixed model, handles text+files only, and first-run setup is non-obvious (GUI editors may not inherit DEEPSEEK_API_KEY from the shell). Polish for real-world use.

## Solution

Wire the unstable session/set_model + availableModels (v4-flash / v4-pro / v4-pro+thinking) for capable clients with DEEPSEEK_MODEL as the default; map ACP image content blocks to DeepSeek image_url parts; write the README including the GUI env-inheritance gotcha.

## User Stories

1. As a Zed user, I want to switch models mid-session, so that I can trade cost/quality per task.
2. As a CodeCompanion user, I want a sensible default model from env, so that it works without a picker.
3. As a user, I want to send images, so that the model can use screenshots/diagrams.
4. As a new user, I want clear setup docs, so that DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is found even when launched from a GUI editor.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a client supporting set_model, then availableModels are advertised and session/set_model switches the active model; otherwise DEEPSEEK_MODEL (default deepseek-v4-pro) is used.
2. Given an ACP prompt with image content, then it is forwarded as an OpenAI-style image_url content part to DeepSeek.
3. Given the README, then it documents env-only config and the agent_servers.env workaround for GUI-launched editors.

## Implementation Notes

- set_model lives behind the crate's unstable feature; a graceful no-op for clients lacking it.

## Testing Notes

- set_model present vs absent; image content mapping.

## Out of Scope

Auto model-router, MCP client support, loadSession.

title: write_file tool (client fs, permission-gated)
description:
## Goal
Let the model create/overwrite files, shown as a native diff.

## Scope
- In: write_file(path, content) via client fs/write_text_file (creates/overwrites); permission-gated; reported via tool_call_update.
- Out: targeted string edits (next task).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. write_file writes through the client fs (editor renders the diff) after permission.
2. Failure (e.g., no fs.writeTextFile capability) returns a clear tool error fed back to the model.

## Testing
- Fake client capturing writes; assert content, permission gating, error path.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate.

title: Permission-posture modes (ask/accept-edits/yolo) + session/set_mode
description:
## Goal
Let the user trade safety for speed via modes.

## Scope
- In: advertise modes in session/new (ask=default, accept-edits, yolo) with currentModeId/availableModes; session/set_mode switches live; mode drives the permission gate (accept-edits auto-allows edits but prompts shell; yolo auto-allows all).
- Out: model selection (M4).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Modes are advertised; session/set_mode changes posture mid-session.
2. accept-edits auto-allows edits, still prompts shell; yolo auto-allows all; ask prompts all mutations.

## Testing
- Matrix: each mode x {edit, shell} asserts prompt-or-not.

## Notes
- Depends on the permission gate.

title: ACP stdio connection & initialize handshake
description:
## Goal
Wire the ACP Agent over stdio and implement the initialize handshake.

## Scope
- In: AgentSideConnection over stdio (agent-client-protocol + -tokio); impl initialize (version negotiate; advertise agent caps: no auth, promptCaps text+embeddedContext+image, loadSession=false); store client capabilities; authenticate as no-op.
- Out: sessions, prompts, tools.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Given an initialize request, the agent negotiates protocol version and returns the advertised capabilities.
2. Client capabilities (fs/terminal) are parsed and stored for later routing.

## Testing
- In-memory fake ACP client sends initialize; assert negotiated version + caps; client caps stored.

## Notes
- Translation boundary: ACP types stay at this layer. Depends on skeleton.
close_reason: Implemented stdio agent bootstrap and initialize handshake

title: Image input: map ACP image blocks to DeepSeek image_url
description:
## Goal
Let users send images to the model.

## Scope
- In: map ACP image content blocks in a prompt to OpenAI-style image_url content parts in the DeepSeek request.
- Out: image generation/output.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. A prompt containing an image block produces a DeepSeek request with a correctly-formatted image_url part.

## Testing
- Unit: ACP image block -> request payload mapping.

## Notes
- Depends on the M0 prompt turn. DeepSeek V4 supports vision.
